audienceSections.normal: Gerard helps Daan and Luuk build a bookcase. You will learn the [[grammar:basic imperative]] and see how separable verbs split in commands and finite main clauses.
audienceSections.expert: [[grammar:`verb stem + remainder`]] is the canonical pattern for give a direct everyday instruction without an expressed subject. `finite stem in V2 ... particle final; infinitive written together` is the canonical pattern for place a separable particle at the clause edge in finite clauses and keep the infinitive together.

audienceSections.normal:
#### basic imperative

Use the verb stem at the beginning to give a direct instruction: `Leg de onderdelen klaar`, `Draai de plank om`, and `Zet de plank terug`. Dutch normally leaves out `jij` in this basic command.

Example: [[grammar:Leg]] eerst alle onderdelen op de tafel [[grammar:klaar]].

Watch out: Do not add `jij` after the command unless you deliberately want a contrastive subject.

#### general separable-verb clause behavior

The dictionary form is one word, such as `klaarleggen` or `omdraaien`. In a finite main clause or command, it splits: `Leg alles klaar` and `Draai de plank om`. After a modal verb, the infinitive stays together: `Moeten we het terugzetten?`.

Example: [[grammar:Leg]] eerst alle onderdelen op de tafel [[grammar:klaar]].

Watch out: Do not keep the particle attached to the finite verb in an ordinary main clause: say `ik zet het terug`, not *`ik terugzet het`.
audienceSections.expert:
#### basic imperative

The unmarked Dutch imperative is morphologically identical to the verb stem and normally lacks an overt subject. It occupies the finite initial position, while complements and separable particles follow. Pragmatic softeners can reduce directness without changing the construction.

Literal chapter example: [[grammar:Leg]] eerst alle onderdelen op de tafel [[grammar:klaar]].

#### general separable-verb clause behavior

A separable particle verb forms a discontinuous predicate in V2 main clauses and imperatives: the finite stem occupies the left bracket and the stressed particle the right bracket. In infinitival environments, the components are written together. Inseparable prefixes do not behave this way.

Literal chapter example: [[grammar:Leg]] eerst alle onderdelen op de tafel [[grammar:klaar]].

audienceSections.normal: Use the [[grammar:verb stem for a basic command]]. With a separable verb, put its particle at the end of the command or finite main clause, but keep the infinitive together.
audienceSections.expert: The [[grammar:unmarked Dutch imperative is morphologically identical to the verb stem]] and normally lacks an overt subject. It occupies the finite initial position, while complements and separable particles follow. Pragmatic softeners can reduce directness without changing the construction. A separable particle verb forms a discontinuous predicate in V2 main clauses and imperatives: the finite stem occupies the left bracket and the stressed particle the right bracket. In infinitival environments, the components are written together. Inseparable prefixes do not behave this way.

breakdown.normal:
- [[grammar:Leg]] eerst alle onderdelen op de tafel [[grammar:klaar]]. — First lay out all the parts on the table.
- [[grammar:Lees]] daarna elke stap in de handleiding zorgvuldig. — Then read every step in the instruction manual carefully.
- Welke plank hoort onderaan? — Which shelf belongs at the bottom?
- Ik zie twee planken die bijna even lang zijn. — I see two shelves that are almost the same length.
- [[grammar:Pak]] de langste plank en draai de plank [[grammar:om]]. — Take the longest shelf and turn it over.
- [[grammar:Controleer]] daarna de nummers bij de gaten. — Then check the numbers by the holes.
- Moet ik de schroeven ook klaarleggen? — Should I lay out the screws too?
- Ik heb de schroevendraaier al gevonden. — I have already found the screwdriver.
- Ja, leg de schroeven naast elk gat. — Yes, put the screws next to each hole.
- [[grammar:Zet]] de waterpas nog even tegen de muur. — Put the spirit level against the wall for a moment.
- De muur is recht, maar de eerste plank staat scheef. — The wall is straight, but the first shelf is crooked.
- Zal ik de plank opnieuw op de tafel leggen? — Shall I put the shelf on the table again?
- [[grammar:Draai]] de schroeven onderaan los en zet de plank [[grammar:terug]]. — Loosen the screws at the bottom and put the shelf back.
- [[grammar:Draai]] ze daarna nog niet helemaal vast. — Do not tighten them completely yet.
- Hoe moet het onderdeel liggen? — How should the part lie?
- De handleiding laat dat niet duidelijk zien. — The instruction manual does not show that clearly.
- [[grammar:Draai]] het onderdeel [[grammar:om]] en kijk naar het nummer. — Turn the part over and look at the number.
- [[grammar:Leg]] het dan met het nummer naar boven. — Then place it with the number facing upward.
- Ik probeer de twee lange onderdelen [[grammar:in elkaar te zetten]]. — I am trying to fit the two long parts together.
- Het lukt nog niet, omdat één gat te klein lijkt. — It is not working yet because one hole seems too small.
- [[grammar:Gebruik]] niet te veel kracht en leg de plank weer op de tafel. — Do not use too much force, and put the shelf back on the table.
- [[grammar:Probeer]] eerst een andere schroef. — Try a different screw first.
- Deze schroef past wel, maar hij zit nog los. — This screw fits, but it is still loose.
- Zal ik hem nu [[grammar:vastdraaien]]? — Shall I tighten it now?
- Ja, draai hem stevig [[grammar:vast]], maar draai niet te hard. — Yes, tighten it firmly, but do not turn too hard.
- [[grammar:Controleer]] daarna met de waterpas of de plank recht staat. — Then use the spirit level to check whether the shelf is straight.
- De boekenkast staat nu recht. — The bookcase is now straight.
- Moeten we de schroeven van de andere plank ook [[grammar:vastdraaien]]? — Do we also need to tighten the screws in the other shelf?
- [[grammar:Draai]] eerst alle schroeven met de hand in de gaten. — First turn all the screws into the holes by hand.
- [[grammar:Gebruik]] de schroevendraaier pas aan het einde. — Use the screwdriver only at the end.
- Eén onderdeel blijft over. — One part is left over.
- Waar moet ik het [[grammar:terugzetten]]? — Where should I put it back?
- [[grammar:Zoek]] het nummer achter in de handleiding [[grammar:op]] en schrijf [[grammar:op]] waar het bij hoort. — Look up the number at the back of the instruction manual and write down what it belongs to.
- [[grammar:Hang]] de waterpas daarna weer aan de muur [[grammar:op]]. — Then hang the spirit level back on the wall.
- Het is gelukt: de boekenkast staat stevig en geen plank beweegt. — It worked: the bookcase is stable, and no shelf moves.
- We zetten de doos [[grammar:terug]] en ruimen de laatste schroeven [[grammar:op]]. — We put the box back and tidy up the remaining screws.
breakdown.expert:
- [[grammar:Leg]] eerst alle onderdelen op de tafel [[grammar:klaar]]. — `Leg` is an unmarked imperative stem with no overt subject; `klaar` is the separated particle in the right bracket. Natural translation: First lay out all the parts on the table.
- [[grammar:Lees]] daarna elke stap in de handleiding zorgvuldig. — `Lees` is an unmarked imperative stem with no overt subject. Natural translation: Then read every step in the instruction manual carefully.
- Welke plank hoort onderaan? — Natural translation: Which shelf belongs at the bottom?
- Ik zie twee planken die bijna even lang zijn. — Natural translation: I see two shelves that are almost the same length.
- [[grammar:Pak]] de langste plank en draai de plank [[grammar:om]]. — `Pak` is an unmarked imperative stem with no overt subject; `om` is the separated particle in the right bracket. Natural translation: Take the longest shelf and turn it over.
- [[grammar:Controleer]] daarna de nummers bij de gaten. — `Controleer` is an unmarked imperative stem with no overt subject. Natural translation: Then check the numbers by the holes.
- Moet ik de schroeven ook klaarleggen? — Natural translation: Should I lay out the screws too?
- Ik heb de schroevendraaier al gevonden. — Natural translation: I have already found the screwdriver.
- Ja, leg de schroeven naast elk gat. — Natural translation: Yes, put the screws next to each hole.
- [[grammar:Zet]] de waterpas nog even tegen de muur. — `Zet` is an unmarked imperative stem with no overt subject. Natural translation: Put the spirit level against the wall for a moment.
- De muur is recht, maar de eerste plank staat scheef. — Natural translation: The wall is straight, but the first shelf is crooked.
- Zal ik de plank opnieuw op de tafel leggen? — Natural translation: Shall I put the shelf on the table again?
- [[grammar:Draai]] de schroeven onderaan los en zet de plank [[grammar:terug]]. — `Draai` is an unmarked imperative stem with no overt subject; `terug` is the separated particle in the right bracket. Natural translation: Loosen the screws at the bottom and put the shelf back.
- [[grammar:Draai]] ze daarna nog niet helemaal vast. — `Draai` is an unmarked imperative stem with no overt subject. Natural translation: Do not tighten them completely yet.
- Hoe moet het onderdeel liggen? — Natural translation: How should the part lie?
- De handleiding laat dat niet duidelijk zien. — Natural translation: The instruction manual does not show that clearly.
- [[grammar:Draai]] het onderdeel [[grammar:om]] en kijk naar het nummer. — `Draai` is an unmarked imperative stem with no overt subject; `om` is the separated particle in the right bracket. Natural translation: Turn the part over and look at the number.
- [[grammar:Leg]] het dan met het nummer naar boven. — `Leg` is an unmarked imperative stem with no overt subject. Natural translation: Then place it with the number facing upward.
- Ik probeer de twee lange onderdelen [[grammar:in elkaar te zetten]]. — `in elkaar te zetten` is a nonfinite form in which the separable verb remains together. Natural translation: I am trying to fit the two long parts together.
- Het lukt nog niet, omdat één gat te klein lijkt. — Natural translation: It is not working yet because one hole seems too small.
- [[grammar:Gebruik]] niet te veel kracht en leg de plank weer op de tafel. — `Gebruik` is an unmarked imperative stem with no overt subject. Natural translation: Do not use too much force, and put the shelf back on the table.
- [[grammar:Probeer]] eerst een andere schroef. — `Probeer` is an unmarked imperative stem with no overt subject. Natural translation: Try a different screw first.
- Deze schroef past wel, maar hij zit nog los. — Natural translation: This screw fits, but it is still loose.
- Zal ik hem nu [[grammar:vastdraaien]]? — `vastdraaien` is a nonfinite form in which the separable verb remains together. Natural translation: Shall I tighten it now?
- Ja, draai hem stevig [[grammar:vast]], maar draai niet te hard. — `vast` is the separated particle in the right bracket. Natural translation: Yes, tighten it firmly, but do not turn too hard.
- [[grammar:Controleer]] daarna met de waterpas of de plank recht staat. — `Controleer` is an unmarked imperative stem with no overt subject. Natural translation: Then use the spirit level to check whether the shelf is straight.
- De boekenkast staat nu recht. — Natural translation: The bookcase is now straight.
- Moeten we de schroeven van de andere plank ook [[grammar:vastdraaien]]? — `vastdraaien` is a nonfinite form in which the separable verb remains together. Natural translation: Do we also need to tighten the screws in the other shelf?
- [[grammar:Draai]] eerst alle schroeven met de hand in de gaten. — `Draai` is an unmarked imperative stem with no overt subject. Natural translation: First turn all the screws into the holes by hand.
- [[grammar:Gebruik]] de schroevendraaier pas aan het einde. — `Gebruik` is an unmarked imperative stem with no overt subject. Natural translation: Use the screwdriver only at the end.
- Eén onderdeel blijft over. — Natural translation: One part is left over.
- Waar moet ik het [[grammar:terugzetten]]? — `terugzetten` is a nonfinite form in which the separable verb remains together. Natural translation: Where should I put it back?
- [[grammar:Zoek]] het nummer achter in de handleiding [[grammar:op]] en schrijf [[grammar:op]] waar het bij hoort. — `Zoek` is an unmarked imperative stem with no overt subject; `op` is the separated particle in the right bracket; `op` is the separated particle in the right bracket. Natural translation: Look up the number at the back of the instruction manual and write down what it belongs to.
- [[grammar:Hang]] de waterpas daarna weer aan de muur [[grammar:op]]. — `Hang` is an unmarked imperative stem with no overt subject; `op` is the separated particle in the right bracket. Natural translation: Then hang the spirit level back on the wall.
- Het is gelukt: de boekenkast staat stevig en geen plank beweegt. — Natural translation: It worked: the bookcase is stable, and no shelf moves.
- We zetten de doos [[grammar:terug]] en ruimen de laatste schroeven [[grammar:op]]. — `terug` is the separated particle in the right bracket; `op` is the separated particle in the right bracket. Natural translation: We put the box back and tidy up the remaining screws.
